summary:
Claim: Maxillary hypoplasia is the primary cause of moon facies in Down syndrome.  
Evidence: - Maxillary hypoplasia leads to reduced midface height and increased facial breadth, creating the characteristic flat, wide appearance. - Mandibular abnormalities (e.g., retrognathia) are minor contributors or absent in most cases.  
Answer: D
